perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party may cause irreparable damage to the Casino and hereby agree that Casino will be entitled to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party may cause irreparable damage to the Casino. The parties agree t hat a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party may cause irreparable damage to the Casino.
perturbation.explanation: Duplicating this sentence while removing the phrase 'hereby agree that Casino will be entitled to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement' removes explicit granting of Casino's ability to injunctive relief and might weaken the legal basis for seeking immediate injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Specific Performance and Remedies (United States)
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the specific reference to injunctive relief, it might be argued that the parties did not explicitly agree to this remedy. While courts can still grant injunctive relief, its omission weakens the Casino's position by removing an explicit acknowledgement of its entitlement to that remedy if irreparable harm is shown. This runs counter to the principle of clearly defining remedies within a contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: specific performance | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you specific performance Specific performance is a contractual remedy in which a court orders a party to fulfill their obligations as closely as possible to what was promised in the contract , rather than simply paying damages for failing to do so. This remedy is often applied when monetary damages are inadequate to fix the harm caused by the breach ; most commonly in cases involving real property and rare chattels . Specific performance ensures that the aggrieved party receives exactly what was agreed upon in the contract, often in situations where the subject matter is unique or irreplaceable. [Last reviewed in June of 2024 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities THE LEGAL PROCESS courts business law contracts wex definitions business sectors civil procedure commercial transactions courts and procedure property law Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the Casino's entitlement to seek injunctive relief. The changed text removes this explicit entitlement, potentially weakening the Casino's legal position. While courts can still grant injunctive relief, the absence of an explicit agreement makes it more challenging for the Casino to secure this remedy. Snippet 1 defines specific performance, a contractual remedy where a court orders a party to fulfill their obligations. By removing the phrase granting the Casino the right to seek injunctive relief, the agreement becomes less specific regarding remedies, moving away from the principle that specific performance ensures the aggrieved party receives what was agreed upon in the contract. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction because the clarity and enforceability of the contract are negatively impacted.
